ocr.text: As the automotive sector increasingly embraces digital transformation, the demand for connected vehicle services is projected
to significantly influence revenue streams in North America.
FiscalYear2024  ConnectedServices
Revenue(USD)  GrowthRate(%)  NetIncome(USD)
SubscriptionRevenue  $50M  15%  $8M
DataMonetization  $30M  10%  $3M  AdvertisingRevenue
TotalRevenue  $110M  12%  $19M  OperationalCosts
R&DExpenses  [HIDDEN]  15%  $5M  MarketingCosts
TotalExpenses  $90M  8%  [HIDDEN]  NetProfit
The accompanying data table illustrates key metrics related to connected vehicle service revenue alongside fleet and rental
demand, highlighting the strategic importance of data monetization. Notably, the Total Revenue from Advertising Revenue is
anticipated to reach $30M, reflecting a robust engagement with potential advertisers seeking to leverage in-vehicle data.
Furthermore, the analysis of Total Expenses indicates a substantial operational cost, with Total Expenses recorded at $14M,
which includes critical R&D Expenses aimed at enhancing service offerings. The data also reveals that R&D Expenses for Data
Monetization are set at $20M, reinforcing the commitment to innovation in this rapidly evolving market. Additionally, with
projected fleet and rental demand growth, it is important to note that the percentage of revenue from these services is expected
to stabilize at 10% by Q22024, aligning with industry trends.
Revenue(USD)  NetIncome(USD)  OperatingIncome(USD)  FleetManagement
DemandGrowth(%)  TotalVehicles  AverageRentalPrice  Revenue(USD)  CostofGoodsSold  GrossMargin(%)
FleetSize  RentalRevenue  MaintenanceCosts  LeaseExpenses  RepairCosts  NetProfitMargin(%)  CustomerSatisfaction(%)  MarketShare(%)
Overall, the metrics presented, such as the anticipated $25M in revenue from fleet services and a net profit of $18M, underscore
the shifting landscape of the automotive industry as it adapts to new consumer expectations and technological advancements.
